{"key_event":"Independence, Partition, and constitutional consolidation","reasoning":"Civil society rebounded strongly through universal franchise, elections, linguistic movements, labor/peasant mobilization, and constitutional rights, though Partition violence, Congress dominance, and preventive detention kept it below a fully constraining level. The state retained the colonial administrative, police, military, and revenue apparatus while gaining sovereignty, integrating princely states, expanding planning, and managing refugees and security crises, producing a very strong but not totalizing state.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":7.6,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.9}
